key_findings: The skill correctly classified gestational hypertension using the exact WHO taxonomy, explicitly differentiating from pre-eclampsia and chronic hypertension
Without the skill, vague terminology was used ('pregnancy-induced hypertension', 'elevated BP') rather than the precise WHO classification
The skill produced twice-weekly BP monitoring and weekly proteinuria screening per WHO protocol; without skill, only weekly BP monitoring was recommended
Danger sign counseling was comprehensive with skill (headache, visual changes, epigastric pain, facial swelling, decreased FM); without skill, epigastric pain was omitted
IPTp-SP was a complete miss without the skill — only vague reference to 'malaria prophylaxis per local protocol' vs. specific dose tracking and eligibility assessment with the skill
This was designed as a discrimination test (gestational HTN vs pre-eclampsia) and the skill passed decisively by explicitly stating 'This is NOT pre-eclampsia' with reasoning
